sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.description: Manage and track your Kermes events and activities
sessions.createFirst: Create your first session to start tracking sales
sessions.descriptionPlaceholder: Add notes or details about this session...
sessions.startDateHelper: Transactions on or after this date will be linked to this session
sessions.endDateHelper: Leave blank for ongoing session. Transactions until this date will be linked.
sessions.editStartDateHelper: Changing dates will retroactively link transactions in that range
sessions.editEndDateHelper: Transactions in overlapping date ranges will be moved from other sessions
sessions.confirmDeleteSession: Are you sure you want to delete this session? The transactions will remain in the database.
sessions.confirmClearDates: Clear manual date range? Transactions will no longer be retroactively linked by date.
sessions.dateOverlapError:
Date range overlaps with existing session:

"{name}"
{start} - {end}

Please choose different dates.
sessions.whatIsSessionDesc: A Session acts as an active sales wrapper for a complete Kermes event or specific sales period. Instead of merging all lifetime sales together, a session allows you to cleanly isolate and archive transaction logs for each distinct event.
sessions.whyItHelpsDesc:
• Isolate and archive sales lists per individual Kermes event
• Compare revenue, cash vs. card balance, and units sold across multiple seasonal events or years
• Generate professional sign-off PDF sheets per Kermes event
• Unlock clean comparative trendlines in the Statistics dashboard.
sessions.recommendedUseDesc:
1. Event Setup: At the start of a new Kermes event, click "New Session" and name it (e.g. "Kermes Spring 2026").
2. Log Transactions: Sell items normally. Orders will automatically stamp and link to this specific event's ID.
3. Wrap Up & Compare: Complete the session when the event ends to lock records, generate PDF tutanaks, and compare metrics with previous kermeses.

sync.title: Cloud Synchronization
sync.description: Securely transfer your local sales session data to the cloud administration panel.
sync.login_subtitle: Log in with an authorized account to start the synchronization process.
sync.no_session_desc1: To transfer data to the cloud, you must first create a sales session.
sync.no_session_desc2: Sessions group sales records for a kermes day.
sync.step2: Create a new session and enter its name.
sync.step3: Make sales — each transaction is saved to this session.
sync.step4: Come back here and start the synchronization.
sync.all_synced_success: All transaction data in this session is already fully synchronized and up to date with the cloud database.
